title: Sandbagging and vanity caps: golf's twin sins
microFact: A padded handicap wins you a trophy once and a reputation forever. Honest posting is non-negotiable.
lesson:
Two ways to lie with a handicap, both fatal to belonging:

SANDBAGGING: keeping your handicap artificially HIGH — not posting good rounds, tanking casual scores — to hoover up net prizes. In club culture this is treated as genuine cheating. Serial net-prize winners get watched, whispered about, and quietly handicap-reviewed by committees (WHS has automatic exceptional-score reductions for this).

VANITY CAPPING: the ego version — keeping it artificially LOW to sound better ("I'm off 18" when you're a 26). Consequence: you're useless to partners in team events and you'll never win anything. Milder sin, still noticed.

Your armor as a newcomer is simple: POST EVERY SCORE. Good, bad, embarrassing — everything, same day. Your Index becomes unimpeachable, your net wins are celebrated instead of side-eyed, and "he posts everything" is among the best reputations a new club golfer can carry.

When you win your first net event off an honest 28 — and you likely will, new golfers improve fast — you'll want that armor on.

quiz.question: What's 'sandbagging'?
options: Carrying extra weight in your bag for training
Keeping your handicap artificially high to win net prizes
quiz.explanation: Inflating your handicap to gain unfair net advantage — golf's most despised offense short of outright cheating.
